# Required GitHub Secrets for releases

The `release.yml` workflow needs these repository secrets configured before the first tagged release. Without them, the workflow runs in stub mode and emits a warning instead of producing artifacts.

| Secret | What it is | How to generate |
|---|---|---|
| `APPLE_DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_P12_BASE64` | Base64 of the Developer ID Application `.p12` export (cert + private key) | Keychain Access → My Certificates → Developer ID → Export `.p12` → `base64 -i cert.p12 -o cert.p12.b64` |
| `APPLE_DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_PASSWORD` | Password set when exporting the `.p12` | (typed at export time) |
| `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD` | Password for the temporary keychain the workflow creates | Any random string |
| `APPLE_ID` | Apple ID email used with notarytool | Your Apple ID |
| `APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD` | App-specific password from appleid.apple.com → Sign-In and Security → App-Specific Passwords | Generate at appleid.apple.com |
| `APPLE_TEAM_ID` | Developer Team ID — usually hardcoded in the workflow env | `9F2JXY8TCK` |
| `SPARKLE_ED_PRIVATE_KEY_BASE64` | Base64 of the EdDSA private key generated by Sparkle | `generate_keys` from Sparkle bundle → `base64 -i sparkle_ed_private.pem -o sparkle_ed_private.pem.b64` |

## Adding secrets

```bash
gh secret set APPLE_DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_P12_BASE64 < cert.p12.b64
gh secret set APPLE_DEVELOPER_ID_CERT_PASSWORD --body "<password>"
gh secret set KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD --body "$(openssl rand -hex 16)"
gh secret set APPLE_ID --body "robertcourson96@gmail.com"
gh secret set APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD --body "<app-specific-password>"
gh secret set SPARKLE_ED_PRIVATE_KEY_BASE64 < sparkle_ed_private.pem.b64
```

The `APPLE_TEAM_ID` is hardcoded in `release.yml` env to `9F2JXY8TCK`. Change it there if your team ID is different.
